tech_news.title: Google will begin punishing sites for back button hijacking in June - Ars Technica
tech_news.description: Google says it could penalize back button hijacking by demoting websites in search ranking.

tech_news.title: Rosalina and Samus have new Nintendo eShop cards, plus an eShop credit sale is live - Nintendo Everything
tech_news.description: New Rosalina and Samus Nintendo eShop cards have been revealed, and a sale is happening that can save you a bit of cash on eShop credit.

Lightweight cabriolet with a high-revving naturally aspirated engine and manual transmission - Porsche Newsroom
tech_news.description: The Porsche GT family welcomes a particularly exciting new member. The 911 GT3 S/C combines a range of ‘driver's car’ qualities that have already delighted customers of the limited-edition 911 Speedster and 911 S/T models.

tech_news.title: Xbox Game Pass finally gets big day-one game after multiple delays - TrueAchievements
tech_news.description: After numerous delays, Replaced finally joins Xbox Game Pass today, and we can now reveal its secretive list of Xbox achievements.

tech_news.title: Anbernic’s new handheld is a pocket-friendly Android device with a swiveling screen - The Verge
tech_news.description: The RG Rotate is a Game Boy clone with a rotating display that shrinks its size.

tech_news.title: ‘Marathon’ Testing A Battle Royale-ish Mode, An Offering To Casuals - Forbes
tech_news.description: Marathon is attempting to extend a hand to casuals by putting all players on the same page for a battle royale type mode where everyone starts on equal footing.

tech_news.title: GoPro goes bigger and pro-er with support for Micro Four Thirds lenses - The Verge
tech_news.description: GoPro’s new Mission 1 cameras use larger 1-inch-type sensors and the new GP3 processor for 8K and 4K open gate filming. And a later Mission 1 Pro ILS model will change lenses.

tech_news.title: Alienware’s new gaming monitor offers a 240Hz QD-OLED panel for just $350 - The Verge
tech_news.description: The Alienware AW2726DM is a 27-inch QD-OLED gaming monitor with QHD resolution and 240Hz refresh. At $350, it’s now one of the most affordable OLEDs around.
